expectations.text: No raw <input>, <select>, or <button> elements. Hearth components (TextInput, Select, Button) used instead.
expectations.evidence: All inputs use TextInput, dropdown uses Select/SelectItem, submit uses Button — all from hearth-react. The <form> element is a structural wrapper, not an input element.

expectations.text: TextInput from hearth-react is used for the full name, email address, and phone number fields.
expectations.evidence: Three Controller-wrapped TextInput components: fullName (type='text'), email (type='email', inputMode='email'), phone (type='tel', inputMode='tel').

expectations.text: Select and SelectItem from hearth-react are used for the preferred contact method dropdown.
expectations.evidence: Select with placeholder='Select a method' contains three SelectItem components: 'email', 'sms', 'post'. Wrapped in Controller with onValueChange={field.onChange}.

expectations.text: Form validation logic is present. Fields show validationStatus='invalid' and validationText with error messages on invalid input.
expectations.evidence: All TextInput fields use validationStatus={fieldState.error ? 'invalid' : undefined} and validationText={fieldState.error?.message}. Rules include required and pattern checks.

expectations.text:
On successful save, an Alert component from hearth-react with colorScheme='positive' (or similar) is shown. Not a raw
or

.
The submit action uses Button from hearth-react, not a raw <button>.
A ContactDetailsForm.test.tsx file is present and contains at least one test.
expectations.evidence: ContactDetailsForm.test.tsx present with 16 tests covering rendering, validation, submission, error handling, dismissal, pre-population, and accessibility.

claims.claim: React Hook Form with Controller and fieldState is used for form management
claims.evidence: Imports useForm, Controller from react-hook-form. Uses Controller render prop with fieldState for each input. mode: 'onTouched' for progressive validation.

claims.claim: fieldsets with legend are used for semantic grouping
claims.evidence: Two <fieldset> elements with <legend> wrapping Heading: 'Personal information' and 'Communication preferences'. CSS resets in ContactDetailsForm.css strip browser default styles.

claims.claim: Button shows loading state during async save
claims.evidence: loading={saveStatus === 'saving'} on Button — prevents double-submit and shows loading indicator.

suggestions.reason: No assertion distinguishes between React Hook Form and manual useState validation. Both satisfy all assertions equally — but one is significantly more robust and scalable. Consider checking for react-hook-form import or Controller usage.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. The with-skill output uses React Hook Form with Controller/fieldState which is the recommended pattern in the skill reference, but the assertions can't distinguish this from the manual-state approach used by the baseline.
